payload.name: 1. Kotlin DSL Deprecation (Highest Priority for Clean
payload.description:
1. Kotlin DSL Deprecation (Highest Priority for Clean Build)
Warning:
'kotlinOptions(KotlinJvmOptionsDeprecated...)' is deprecated. Please migrate to the compilerOptions DSL.
Best Fix:
Update these files (they are in your node_modules but you can override):
Create or update android/build.gradle or the specific plugin files:
Kotlin// Instead of kotlinOptions { ... }
kotlin {
    compilerOptions {
        jvmTarget.set(JvmTarget.JVM_17)  // or JVM_21
        // other options
    }
}
Quick command to check affected files:
Bashgrep -r "kotlinOptions" node_modules/expo* node_modules/react-native*
2. Manifest Package Attribute Warnings (Security + Cleanliness)
Warnings:

package="com.reactnativecommunity.asyncstorage"
package="com.th3rdwave.safeareacontext"

Best Fix:
These are library-side issues. Force a clean manifest by adding this to your android/app/build.gradle:
gradleandroid {
    namespace 'com.anonymous.arete'  // Use your actual app package name
    
    defaultConfig {
        manifestPlaceholders = [packageName: "com.anonymous.arete"]
    }
}
Then clean and rebuild:
Bashcd android && ./gradlew clean && cd .. && npx expo run:android --variant release
3. React Native Screens + Edge-to-Edge Deprecations (SDK 35+)
Many warnings about status bar, navigation bar, translucent, etc.
Best Fix:
Update react-native-screens to latest version and configure edge-to-edge properly in your MainActivity.kt or AndroidManifest.xml:
XML<!-- android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml -->
<application
    android:enableOnBackInvokedCallback="true"
    ... >
In your root component, use expo-status-bar or react-native-screens latest APIs instead of deprecated props.
4. ReactNativeHost Deprecations (Architecture Migration)
Best Long-term Fix (Recommended for Quality & Future-proofing):
Migrate toward New Architecture (Fabric + TurboModules).
Steps:

Update app.json / app.config.js:

JSON{
  "expo": {
    "newArchEnabled": true
  }
}

Update all libraries to versions that support New Architecture.

5. General Best Cleanup Commands (Run in Order)
Bash# 1. Clean caches
npx expo clean
cd android && ./gradlew clean

# 2. Update everything
npx expo upgrade
npm install   # or yarn install

# 3. Rebuild
npx expo run:android --variant release
Security & Quality Recommendations (Non-Negotiable)

Target SDK 36 is good — keep it.
Use minSdk 24 (already set) — acceptable.
Keep ProGuard/R8 enabled (default in release).
After building, scan the APK with:Bash./gradlew :app:lintRelease
Always sign with proper keystore (don't use debug keys for release).

goal.answer: Eliminate Android deprecation warnings (kotlinOptions DSL, library manifest `package=` attrs, edge-to-edge props), confirm New Architecture is enabled, configure proper release signing via env vars, and verify with a clean `./gradlew :app:assembleRelease` that exits 0 with zero matches for the four target warning patterns.

payload.plain: Eliminate Android deprecation warnings (kotlinOptions DSL, library manifest `package=` attrs, edge-to-edge props), confirm New Architecture is enabled, configure proper release signing via env vars, and verify with a clean `./gradlew :app:assembleRelease` that exits 0 with zero matches for the four target warning patterns.

criteria.answer:
**Kotlin DSL migrated**: `grep -rn "kotlinOptions" android/ app/ patches/` returns no matches in active sources; patches/ contains diffs for react-native-worklets, expo-modules-autolinking (3 sub-plugins), and expo-modules-core; `npx patch-package` applies them via postinstall.
**Manifest clean**: `android/app/build.gradle` sets `manifestPlaceholders = [packageName: "com.anonymous.arete"]`; `./gradlew :app:assembleRelease` log has zero matches for `package="com\.` (library-side warning silenced).
**Gradle properties deprecation removed**: `expo.edgeToEdgeEnabled` line deleted from `android/gradle.properties` (it self-declares deprecated and removed in SDK 55); `edgeToEdgeEnabled=true` retained.
**New Arch verified**: `newArchEnabled=true` confirmed in `android/gradle.properties`; audit note `docs/new-arch-audit.md` lists each installed library's New Arch support status with source links.
**Release keystore configured**: `android/app/release.keystore` exists (10-year validity, RSA-2048); `android/keystore.properties` listed in `.gitignore`; `android/keystore.properties.example` committed with placeholder values; `signingConfigs.release` reads `System.getenv("ARETE_RELEASE_STORE_PASSWORD")` etc. and fails fast with a clear error message if unset.
**Clean release build**: `cd android && ./gradlew :app:assembleRelease --no-daemon 2>&1 | tee /tmp/release-build.log` exits 0; `grep -cE "(kotlinOptions|ReactiveNat|statusBarStyle|translucent|navigationBar|predictiveBack|package=\"com\.)" /tmp/release-build.log` returns 0.
**Debug sanity**: `cd android && ./gradlew :app:assembleDebug --no-daemon` exits 0 (signingConfig split did not break debug).

plain: **Kotlin DSL migrated**: `grep -rn "kotlinOptions" android/ app/ patches/` returns no matches in active sources; patches/ contains diffs for react-native-worklets, expo-modules-autolinking (3 sub-plugins), and expo-modules-core; `npx patch-package` applies them via postinstall.

**Manifest clean**: `android/app/build.gradle` sets `manifestPlaceholders = [packageName: "com.anonymous.arete"]`; `./gradlew :app:assembleRelease` log has zero matches for `package="com\.` (library-side warning silenced).

**Gradle properties deprecation removed**: `expo.edgeToEdgeEnabled` line deleted from `android/gradle.properties` (it self-declares deprecated and removed in SDK 55); `edgeToEdgeEnabled=true` retained.
**New Arch verified**: `newArchEnabled=true` confirmed in `android/gradle.properties`; audit note `docs/new-arch-audit.md` lists each installed library's New Arch support status with source links.

**Release keystore configured**: `android/app/release.keystore` exists (10-year validity, RSA-2048); `android/keystore.properties` listed in `.gitignore`; `android/keystore.properties.example` committed with placeholder values; `signingConfigs.release` reads `System.getenv("ARETE_RELEASE_STORE_PASSWORD")` etc. and fails fast with a clear error message if unset.

**Clean release build**: `cd android && ./gradlew :app:assembleRelease --no-daemon 2>&1 | tee /tmp/release-build.log` exits 0; `grep -cE "(kotlinOptions|ReactiveNat|statusBarStyle|translucent|navigationBar|predictiveBack|package=\"com\.)" /tmp/release-build.log` returns 0.
**Debug sanity**: `cd android && ./gradlew :app:assembleDebug --no-daemon` exits 0 (signingConfig split did not break debug).

constraints.answer: Expo SDK 54 / React Native 0.81.5 — do NOT run `expo upgrade` (out of scope; project intentionally pinned)., Target SDK 54 docs for any version-specific guidance (NOT v57 per the AGENTS.md hint, which is incorrect for this project)., No new runtime dependencies; only `patch-package` as a devDependency., Do not modify `package.json` library versions (no `npm install <pkg>@latest`)., Android-only; iOS, web, and JS code untouched., Release keystore credentials MUST be read from env vars, never committed in plain text., Do not patch `react-native/ReactAndroid/build.gradle.kts` — too large, drifts on minor bumps; document as pending upstream fix instead.

plain: Expo SDK 54 / React Native 0.81.5 — do NOT run `expo upgrade` (out of scope; project intentionally pinned).
Target SDK 54 docs for any version-specific guidance (NOT v57 per the AGENTS.md hint, which is incorrect for this project).
No new runtime dependencies; only `patch-package` as a devDependency.
Do not modify `package.json` library versions (no `npm install <pkg>@latest`).
Android-only; iOS, web, and JS code untouched.
Release keystore credentials MUST be read from env vars, never committed in plain text.
Do not patch `react-native/ReactAndroid/build.gradle.kts` — too large, drifts on minor bumps; document as pending upstream fix instead.

phases.answer: Tooling & Release Signing, Kotlin DSL Patches, Manifest & Gradle Properties Cleanup, New Arch Audit & Release Verification

phaseSnapshots.name: Tooling & Release Signing
phaseSnapshots.goal: Install patch-package, generate a release keystore with env-var credential resolution, and wire signingConfigs so the debug variant is unaffected.
phaseSnapshots.description: Foundation phase: establish the tooling (patch-package) needed by phase 2, and the signing infrastructure needed by phase 4.

steps.description: Install patch-package as a devDependency: `npm install --save-dev patch-package`. Add `"postinstall": "patch-package"` to package.json scripts. Verify with `npx patch-package --help` exiting 0.

Generate release keystore non-interactively: `keytool -genkeypair -v -keystore /Users/shaz/Desktop/arete/android/app/release.keystore -alias arete-release -keyalg RSA -keysize 2048 -validity 3650 -storepass "$ARETE_RELEASE_STORE_PASSWORD" -keypass "$ARETE_RELEASE_KEY_PASSWORD" -dname "CN=Arete,OU=Mobile,O=Anonymous,L=Internal,ST=NA,C=US"`. Use a fixed test password locally for first generation; document env var requirement in keystore.properties.example.

Create `android/keystore.properties.example` with placeholder values (`storeFile=release.keystore`, `storePassword=__SET_ARETE_RELEASE_STORE_PASSWORD__`, `keyAlias=arete-release`, `keyPassword=__SET_ARETE_RELEASE_KEY_PASSWORD__`). Add `android/keystore.properties` to `.gitignore`. Do NOT commit the real `keystore.properties`.

Edit `android/app/build.gradle` to (a) add a `keystoreProperties` loader at top of file that reads `keystore.properties` if present, (b) add `signingConfigs.release` block using those properties, (c) update `buildTypes.release.signingConfig` to reference `signingConfigs.release` (remove the debug keystore reuse). If `keystore.properties` is missing AND env vars are unset, fail with a clear `throw new GradleException("Set ARETE_RELEASE_STORE_PASSWORD env var or create android/keystore.properties")`.
Sanity: debug build still works after signingConfig refactor. Run `cd /Users/shaz/Desktop/arete/android && ./gradlew :app:assembleDebug --no-daemon`. Must exit 0.

phaseSnapshots.name: Kotlin DSL Patches
phaseSnapshots.goal: Eliminate `kotlinOptions` deprecation warnings from transitive Gradle plugins in node_modules by creating patch-package diffs and wiring postinstall to re-apply them.
phaseSnapshots.description: Targets the highest-priority warning (kotlinOptions) from 5 known sources in node_modules. Uses patch-package rather than source-tree edits because the source files are regenerated on every `npm install`.

steps.description: Patch `react-native-worklets`: edit `node_modules/react-native-worklets/android/build.gradle` line ~298 to replace the deprecated `kotlinOptions { jvmTarget = "..." }` block with the modern Kotlin 1.9+ DSL: `kotlin { compilerOptions { jvmTarget.set(org.jetbrains.kotlin.gradle.dsl.JvmTarget.JVM_17) } }`. Then run `npx patch-package react-native-worklets` to generate `patches/react-native-worklets+<version>.patch`.

Patch `expo-modules-autolinking`: edit the 3 sub-plugin build.gradle.kts files (`expo-autolinking-plugin`, `expo-autolinking-settings-plugin`, `expo-autolinking-plugin-shared`) to migrate `kotlinOptions { ... }` blocks to `kotlin { compilerOptions { ... } }` using the `kotlinOptions` extension's replacement API. Run `npx patch-package expo-modules-autolinking` to generate a single combined patch.

Patch `expo-modules-core`: edit `node_modules/expo-modules-core/expo-module-gradle-plugin/build.gradle.kts` line ~58 to migrate `kotlinOptions { ... }` to `kotlin { compilerOptions { ... } }`. Run `npx patch-package expo-modules-core`.

Document the deferred `react-native/ReactAndroid/build.gradle.kts` patch: create `patches/README.md` explaining why RN core is NOT patched (drift risk on version bumps), and link to the upstream React Native issue for the kotlinOptions migration. Do not edit RN core.

Verify postinstall re-applies all patches cleanly: rm -rf node_modules and run `npm install` (simulates fresh install). Then `npx patch-package` should report zero patches to apply (they're already applied) OR re-apply successfully. Final check: `grep -c "kotlinOptions" /Users/shaz/Desktop/arete/node_modules/react-native-worklets/android/build.gradle` should return 0 in any of the patched files.

phaseSnapshots.name: Manifest & Gradle Properties Cleanup
phaseSnapshots.goal: Silence the `package=` manifest warnings from third-party libraries and remove the self-deprecated `expo.edgeToEdgeEnabled` property.
phaseSnapshots.description: Addresses items #2 (manifest package attrs) and #3 (edge-to-edge deprecations) from the user's intent. The deprecated gradle.properties entry is a separate, easily-fixed source of build noise.

steps.description: Edit `android/app/build.gradle` `defaultConfig` block to add `manifestPlaceholders = [packageName: "com.anonymous.arete"]`. This is the documented AGP suppression path for legacy `package=` attrs in library manifests being merged into the app.

Remove the deprecated line `expo.edgeToEdgeEnabled=true` from `android/gradle.properties`. Keep `edgeToEdgeEnabled=true` (the supported property). The deprecated line's own comment notes removal in SDK 55.

Document `predictiveBackGestureEnabled: false` in `app.json`: add a comment-style property (or note in PR description) explaining this is intentional because the current ReactActivity + react-native-screens 4.x combination does not yet support predictive back on Android 14+ without flicker, and is disabled to avoid UX regressions.

phaseSnapshots.name: New Arch Audit & Release Verification
phaseSnapshots.goal: Confirm New Architecture is fully enabled and verify the entire build is clean by running :app:assembleRelease and grepping the log for the four target warning patterns.
phaseSnapshots.description: Final phase: audit item #4 (no code change needed; just documentation) and produce the proof-of-clean-build evidence called for in the success criteria.

steps.description: Write `docs/new-arch-audit.md` listing each installed library from `package.json` (expo, react, react-native, @react-native-async-storage/async-storage, @react-navigation/native, expo-file-system, expo-notifications, expo-sharing, expo-sqlite, expo-status-bar, react-native-gesture-handler, react-native-reanimated, react-native-safe-area-context, react-native-screens, zustand) with a per-row New Arch support status (Yes / Partial / Pending) and source link (GitHub repo or react-native-compatibility matrix).

Confirm `newArchEnabled=true` in gradle.properties and `hermesEnabled=true` (already set, sanity check). Add `newArchEnabled: true` to `app.json` `expo` block if not present (defensive double-bind for future tooling).

Run the canonical verification: `cd /Users/shaz/Desktop/arete/android && ./gradlew :app:assembleRelease --no-daemon 2>&1 | tee /tmp/release-build.log`. Then grep for the four target patterns: `grep -cE "(kotlinOptions|ReactiveNat|statusBarStyle|translucent|navigationBar|predictiveBack|package=\"com\.)" /tmp/release-build.log` must return 0. Build must exit 0.

Final debug sanity check: `cd /Users/shaz/Desktop/arete/android && ./gradlew :app:assembleDebug --no-daemon` must still exit 0 (ensures the signingConfig split and patch-package integration didn't regress the common dev workflow).

assumptions.answer: This ferment targets Expo SDK 54 (not v57 as the AGENTS.md heading implies). The AGP 8+ `kotlinOptions` deprecation originates from transitive Gradle plugins in node_modules (`react-native-worklets`, `expo-modules-autolinking` x3, `expo-modules-core`); `patch-package` is the standard fix and is already battle-tested in the React Native ecosystem. The app's own `AndroidManifest.xml` already omits the legacy `package=` attribute (good), so the remaining `package=` warnings come from third-party library manifests (async-storage 2.2.0, safe-area-context 5.6.0); `manifestPlaceholders = [packageName: "..."]` in `app/build.gradle` is the documented AGP suppression path (we won't bump library versions to chase upstream fixes). The `expo.edgeToEdgeEnabled` property in `gradle.properties` self-declares as deprecated and slated for removal in SDK 55, so deletion is safe and aligned with the project's own deprecation guidance. `newArchEnabled=true` is already set in `gradle.properties`, so item #4 reduces to a compatibility audit, not a code change. Release keystore generation via `keytool -genkeypair` is non-interactive when all flags (storepass, keypass, dname) are supplied, so this can run unattended. The release variant currently reuses the debug keystore (`signingConfig signingConfigs.debug` for release), which is acceptable for development but must be replaced before any production distribution. Patches will be regenerated via `postinstall` script on every `npm install`; users adding new transitive plugins that still use `kotlinOptions` should re-run `npx patch-package <pkg>` and commit the new diff.
